title: Add few-shot examples to agent output format prompts
source: Anthropic prompt engineering docs — few-shot examples improve structured output reliability by 40-60% vs instruction-only prompts
description: The current agent prompts describe the expected JSON output format but provide no concrete few-shot example of a completed agent response. Adding 1-2 realistic examples showing the full ANALYZE→PLAN→VERIFY→EXECUTE reasoning followed by the JSON report block would significantly reduce malformed outputs and improve first-attempt parsing success. This is especially impactful for sonnet-tier models which benefit more from examples than from lengthy instructions.
proposed_changes: Add a new <examples> XML section between </reasoning_protocol> and <execution_rules> (around line 671) in the prompt assembly. Include 1-2 brief, realistic examples showing: (1) a short ANALYZE/PLAN block, (2) a code change description, (3) the correctly formatted JSON report block. Use <example> and </example> sub-tags. Keep examples short (~15 lines each) to avoid bloating the prompt. Also add a minimal example to the Meeseeks prompt in meeseeks.py after the JSON format specification (line 117) showing a completed simple task report.
